violations.text: Baby formula was held past the use by date on the can. PIC took product off shelf to get credit for formula. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the milk cooler shelving have an accumulation of dust and bio-film. Shelf to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2025

Several Dairy Items in the Retail reach-In Cooler were observed held at 46 to 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Items observed at 44 degrees Fahrenheit and below were moved to colder refrigeration at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/03/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the retail reach-in refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2024

At the time of inspection, the retail reach-in cooler containing dairy food items was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 37 to 47 degrees Fahrenheit with food temperatures observed held at 44 to 47 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC set up a ticket for service/repair to the reach-in cooler at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/17/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the dairy reach-in cooler was observed to have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris below the eggs and milk bottom shelf. Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 01/06/2024
